summary:
Overall UK risk remains Elevated. Economic pressure points persist: headline CPI inflation is running at 3.3% (March 2026) and was driven higher month-on-month by transport costs (motor fuels), leaving the outlook sensitive to further moves in oil and other traded commodities.

Security and resilience risks also remain elevated. Russia’s ongoing long-range strikes in Ukraine continue to target both energy infrastructure and Black Sea-linked logistics, sustaining European escalation and energy-market spillover risk. Domestically, UK cyber posture remains exposed to widely deployed edge/perimeter vulnerabilities, while near-term natural-hazard risk appears relatively contained with no active Met Office weather warnings and lower Environment Agency flood alert counts versus the prior day.

categories.name: Economic / Financial
categories.rationale: Economic risk remains Elevated: ONS data show CPI inflation at 3.3% in the 12 months to March 2026 (up from 3.0% in February), with transport (motor fuels) a key upward driver, keeping the cost-of-living and policy backdrop sensitive to energy-price volatility.
drivers: ONS: CPI inflation 3.3% (12 months to March 2026), up from 3.0% in February; transport (motor fuels) was a key upward driver. https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/march2026
Trading Economics: Brent crude oil about 106.03 USD/bbl (Apr 24, 2026). https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil

Ofgem: energy price cap for 1 Apr–30 Jun 2026 is £1,641/year (typical dual-fuel household paying by Direct Debit). https://www.ofgem.gov.uk/information-consumers/energy-advice-households/energy-price-cap-explained
OBR (March 2026 EFO): real GDP growth forecast 1.1% in 2026 (central forecast). https://obr.uk/efo/economic-and-fiscal-outlook-march-2026/

categories.name: Military / Security
categories.rationale: European security remains Elevated due to ongoing Russia–Ukraine conflict activity and associated escalation/spillover risk; UK domestic terrorism threat level remains SUBSTANTIAL.
drivers: Reuters: Russia hit port infrastructure in Ukraine’s Odesa region and an energy installation in Chernihiv region; outages affected ~380,000 residents (18 Apr 2026). https://www.reuters.com/world/europe/russia-hits-port-power-facility-ukraine-overnight-2026-04-18/
MI5: UK national terrorism threat level is SUBSTANTIAL (an attack is likely). https://www.mi5.gov.uk/threats-and-advice/terrorism-threat-levels

categories.name: Political / Governance
categories.rationale: No acute UK governance instability signal identified in reviewed official sources; government communications continue to emphasise cost-of-living support and management of external spillovers.
drivers: GOV.UK: new measures (from 1 Apr 2026) intended to ease cost-of-living pressure, including energy-bill reductions and wage increases. https://www.gov.uk/government/news/new-measures-coming-in-to-ease-cost-of-living-pressure-1-april-2026

categories.name: Natural Hazards
categories.rationale: Natural-hazard risk remains Guarded and appears to be easing: the Met Office shows no active UK weather warnings and the England national flood snapshot shows fewer alerts than the prior update.
drivers: Met Office: no active UK weather warnings listed (viewed 24 Apr 2026). https://weather.metoffice.gov.uk/warnings-and-advice/uk-warnings
Environment Agency (GOV.UK): England national snapshot shows 0 flood warnings and 2 flood alerts (viewed 24 Apr 2026). https://check-for-flooding.service.gov.uk

categories.name: Social Stability
categories.rationale: Social disruption risk remains Elevated, with ongoing London Underground industrial action continuing to generate significant commuter and knock-on network disruption risk.
drivers: The Independent: reporting on London Tube strike dates and expected disruption (updated 22 Apr 2026). https://www.the-independent.com/travel/news-and-advice/london-tube-strikes-dates-2026-b2962428.html
TfL: real-time status for Tube/DLR/Overground during strike periods. https://tfl.gov.uk/tube-dlr-overground/status/

categories.name: Critical Infrastructure / Cyber
categories.rationale: Cyber/CNI risk remains Elevated due to urgent patching requirements for widely deployed perimeter appliances (NetScaler) and ongoing risk of exploitation of edge services.
drivers: NCSC: urges immediate action to mitigate Citrix NetScaler ADC/Gateway vulnerabilities CVE-2026-3055 and CVE-2026-4368. https://www.ncsc.gov.uk/news/vulnerabilities-affecting-citrix-netscaler-adc-gateway

Citrix: security bulletin for NetScaler ADC/Gateway vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-3055 and CVE-2026-4368) with fixed versions. https://support.citrix.com/external/article/CTX696300/netscaler-adc-and-netscaler-gateway-secu.html

indicators.name: Brent crude
indicators.note: Trading Economics shows Brent crude oil at about 106.03 USD/bbl (Apr 24, 2026). https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil

indicators.name: UK CPI inflation (12 months to Mar 2026)
indicators.note: ONS: CPI rose by 3.3% in the 12 months to March 2026, up from 3.0% in February; transport (motor fuels) was the largest upward contribution. https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/march2026

indicators.name: Ofgem energy price cap (typical dual-fuel, Direct Debit)
indicators.note: Ofgem: price cap for 1 Apr–30 Jun 2026 is £1,641/year, down from £1,758 in 1 Jan–31 Mar 2026. https://www.ofgem.gov.uk/information-consumers/energy-advice-households/energy-price-cap-explained

indicators.name: UK terrorism threat level
indicators.note: MI5: UK national terrorism threat level is SUBSTANTIAL (attack is likely). https://www.mi5.gov.uk/threats-and-advice/terrorism-threat-levels

indicators.name: England flood warnings/alerts (national snapshot)
indicators.note: Environment Agency: England national snapshot lists 0 flood warnings and 2 flood alerts (viewed 24 Apr 2026). https://check-for-flooding.service.gov.uk

changes_since_last_update: Timestamp advanced to 24 April 2026 05:00 UK; overall status held at Elevated.
Brent crude indicator refreshed to ~106.03 USD/bbl (Trading Economics, 24 Apr 2026).
Environment Agency national snapshot updated: 0 flood warnings / 2 flood alerts (down from 1 / 3), supporting an improving Natural Hazards trend.
Ofgem price cap indicator noted as lower for Apr–Jun 2026 versus Jan–Mar 2026, reinforcing a modest near-term easing in household energy-bill pressure.

Economic / Financial trend moved from worsening to unchanged: no new macro release beyond the latest published CPI reading was identified in this run; inflation remains elevated but the direction signal is not materially changed versus the prior day’s assessment.
priority_watch_items: Energy/oil sensitivity: monitor whether further Brent moves feed through into UK pump prices and near-term inflation expectations.
London transport disruption: monitor service impacts through the tail of current industrial action and any further announced strike dates.
Perimeter vulnerability exposure: confirm remediation/upgrade progress for NetScaler ADC/Gateway and monitor for exploitation indicators.
European security: monitor for further Russia–Ukraine escalation affecting energy systems and Black Sea-linked logistics.
Flooding: monitor Environment Agency updates for any rapid escalation in local warnings/alerts following rainfall.
source_notes: This update prioritises UK official sources (ONS, Ofgem, MI5, NCSC, Environment Agency, Met Office, TfL, GOV.UK) with Reuters used for conflict reporting; Trading Economics is used for a Brent price snapshot. https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil

EA national flood snapshot extraction did not reliably return the page’s explicit update timestamp in this run; counts are from the live national snapshot view on 24 Apr 2026. https://check-for-flooding.service.gov.uk
Some items (e.g., transport disruption) depend on live operational status pages; conditions can change intraday. https://tfl.gov.uk/tube-dlr-overground/status/
